I half expected he’d start when I spoke, but he didn’t: he looked round slowly, but with a haunted look in his eyes as if I might have been one of his ghosts. He was a tall man, gaunt and haggard-eyed, as many men are in the bush; he may have been but little past middle age, and grey before his time. “Good day,” he said, and he set the stone in its place, carefully flush with the outer edge of the wall, before he spoke again..
